creator: alexlapugeanfbsd@outlook.com
text:
Hello,

I have two Dell laptops with a 4 pin headset jack (Dell E6440 and Dell E6540). Jack sensing detects the headset speakers but not the headset microphone, the headset microphone is not working.

I tried to change sources in the mixer but to no avail.

In linux, the headset microphone is associated with NID 0x1a (26), and the entire headset works without issues.

In FreeBSD, NID26 is marked as a SPEAKER even though it's not, I changed it thru device hints to Mic.

The last issue is white noise in the headset speakers whenever the igain for the onboard microphone  (NID18) is not 0, even setting it to just 1 starts the white noise again.


cat /boot/device.hints
hint.hdac.1.cad0.nid18.config="as=3"
hint.hdac.1.cad0.nid22.config="as=0"
hint.hdac.1.cad0.nid25.config="as=0"
hint.hdac.1.cad0.nid26.config="as=4 device=Mic conn=Jack"
hint.hdac.0.cad0.nid6.config="as=0"
hint.hdac.0.cad0.nid7.config="as=0"


Listing configuration:
sysctl hw.snd.default_unit
hw.snd.default_unit: 0

sysctl dev.hdaa
dev.hdaa.1.init_clear: 0
dev.hdaa.1.reconfig: 0
dev.hdaa.1.gpo_config:
dev.hdaa.1.gpo_state:

dev.hdaa.1.gpio_state: 0=disabled 1=disabled 2=disabled 3=disabled 4=disabled
dev.hdaa.1.gpi_state:
dev.hdaa.1.config: forcestereo,ivref50,ivref80,ivref100,ivref,vref
